finding_results.notes: Reworked Derek exchange to introduce money friction: Derek is $10 short (folded twenty instead of thirty), creating an operational consequence Marcus must manage — he covers the shortfall himself rather than report it to Harry, who would want to drop the buyer. This changes what Marcus has to do next (absorb the loss, conceal it from Harry's ledger), satisfying the acceptance test's requirement that at least one conversation create friction with downstream behavioral consequence. Removed Derek's thesis line ('I'm not trying to hustle the system or anything. I just need my meds.') and replaced the exchange's closing with messier, pressure-driven speech: Derek's wife missing work, his trailing-off frustration ('Whatever. Forget it.'), and his apology for the shortfall. The three exchanges now have distinct architectures: Rosa is smooth and businesslike (routine trust), Derek is friction-heavy and unresolved (money problem, sumatriptan ask, operational consequence), Janine is demand-driven with dark humor (CVS price check, schedule change request, dog's teeth). No buyer line now functions as the chapter's moral thesis. Updated money count ($200 from $210), Janine's envelope detail (red), and final-paragraph memory references to match revised Derek exchange.

summary: Single finding addressed: the three parking-lot buyer conversations no longer share the same polite disclosure-and-exit turn-shape. Derek's exchange was substantially reworked to introduce money friction ($10 short) that creates real operational consequence for Marcus — he must cover the shortfall and hide it from Harry, who tracks every dollar. Derek's thesis-stating line ('I just need my meds') was removed; the exchange now ends on the unresolved note of Derek's apology and Marcus's decision to protect him. Rosa's exchange remains the smooth, businesslike baseline. Janine's exchange retains its distinct demand-driven shape (schedule change, CVS price complaint). Downstream references (money count, final paragraph) updated for consistency. No new continuity issues introduced; chapter heading contract preserved.
